title: India Condemns Attack on Settebello Off Oman Coast; Three Crew Missing
lede: India's Ministry of External Affairs condemned an attack on the commercial vessel Settebello off Oman's coast on June 10, 2026, with 21 of 24 Indian crew rescued and three reported missing, as New Delhi calls for regional de-escalation.

sections.context: The attack on the Settebello represents another incident in a pattern of maritime violence affecting commercial shipping in the region. India's official statement characterizes these continuing attacks as a "direct result of the ongoing conflict in the region," positioning the incident within a broader security crisis affecting international waterways. The Indian Embassy in Oman mobilized immediately to coordinate search and rescue operations with Omani authorities, demonstrating active diplomatic engagement in a humanitarian emergency. The incident underscores the vulnerability of Indian-flagged and Indian-crewed vessels transiting regional waters, with 24 Indian nationals aboard the Settebello at the time of attack.
sections.threads: This attack follows a pattern of regional maritime incidents that have drawn India's diplomatic attention and formal condemnation. [[india-om-india-condemns-attack-on-settebello-regional-shipping-c-2026|Previous attacks on shipping in the region]] have prompted similar statements from New Delhi emphasizing the need for de-escalation. The incident occurs amid a broader strategic context where India has been deepening engagement with Gulf states; [[india-om-pm-modis-december-2025-oman-visit-strengthens-india-gul-2026|PM Modi's December 2025 Oman visit]] and subsequent diplomatic activity reflect India's efforts to strengthen bilateral relations and regional stability. The targeting of commercial vessels carrying Indian crew highlights the human cost and economic implications of regional instability on Indian nationals and maritime commerce.
sections.signal: India's statement explicitly calls for "immediate de-escalation of tensions, and the conclusion of ongoing negotiations for a diplomatic solution," signaling New Delhi's prioritization of regional conflict resolution over the coming months. The restoration of "free and unimpeded navigation and commerce through the international waterways in the region in keeping with international law" emerges as a key Indian objective, suggesting potential diplomatic engagement on maritime security frameworks. Analysts should monitor whether India coordinates multilaterally on shipping security measures, lodges formal protests through international bodies, or escalates engagement with Omani and other regional authorities on establishing shipping protection protocols. The fate of the three missing Indian crew members and any subsequent official findings on the attack's perpetrators will likely shape India's next diplomatic moves.
